guest_role: Co-founder and CEO at Science Inc. (launching Science Blockchain, first ICO blockchain incubator); ex-CEO of MySpace
assertiveness_why: Harry imports outside VCs' framings (Andy at USV, an unnamed guest's 'Filecoin is the landmark' binary thesis) and asks Jones to agree or disagree, which is a mild form of pressure-testing, but he never independently disputes a claim himself.
question_style: Dense, well-briefed sequential questions that build a taxonomy (utility vs securitized tokens) and periodically pose a third party's competing view for the guest to react to; no interruptions, largely deferential once Jones is answering.
tone: Warm and familiar (second appearance, 'I so enjoyed the first time'), high energy and genuinely curious about a fast-moving technical topic, ends with a friendly bitcoin-price wager.
depth: Highly technical for the era: utility vs. securitized tokens, SEC crowdfunding compliance, token-flow-through incubator structuring, M&A implications of tokenized cap tables, and geographic capital flows into ICOs.
harry_quotes: Do you agree with the roster, the kind of binary nature?
What are the companies it makes sense for, and what does it not?
Is it a substitute? Is it a compliment?
learnings: Jones distinguishes utility tokens (akin to an always-on global domain-name auction) from securitized tokens structured to comply with SEC crowdfunding rules.
Science Blockchain's structure passes portfolio companies' own tokens directly through to fund token-holders' wallets, giving LPs individual control absent in a traditional fund structure.
He sees three waves of capital entering crypto: early true-believer miners, then venture/tech insiders, then traditional LPs and family offices now moving in via trusted managers.
Tokenization only makes sense for businesses needing irrevocable digital ownership transfer, decentralized trust, or global value transfer — not as a universal fundraising replacement.
He flags an unresolved conflict for acquirers: how a Google or Facebook would handle acquiring a company whose token still floats on public exchanges post-acquisition.
repeat_guest_callback: Mike, it's such a pleasure to have you on the show for round two. What can I say? I so enjoyed the first time that I had to have you back for the very exciting news.
notable: Episode is a real-time snapshot of peak 2017 ICO mania, timed to launch 13 days ahead of an actual token presale, with a closing friendly bet on Bitcoin's price a year out.
